title: Rails conventions and Zeitwerk autoloading
description: Rails maps class names to file paths via Zeitwerk and infers tables, routes, and associations from naming conventions.
body: Use when adding code or debugging a load error. Rails uses Zeitwerk to autoload constants by mapping a CamelCase class to a snake_case file at the expected path, so Users::Profile must live in app/models/users/profile.rb. Conventions also infer the table name (pluralized), foreign keys, and RESTful routes from names. A mismatch (wrong file path, unexpected acronym inflection) yields a NameError or 'expected file to define constant' at boot. Do not work around autoload errors with manual require. Actions: align file path, class name, and inflections; register custom acronyms. Validation signals include the constant autoloading and the route resolving in a spec. Risk boundary: fighting convention with manual requires creates load-order fragility that breaks on Rails upgrades; rename to fit convention instead.
